title: Benkara miquelii
taxonomy: Benkara miquelii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to southern China and Vietnam. The plant belongs to the genus Benkara, which is a part of the Rubiaceae family. The classification of Benkara miquelii places it within the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. Further classification includes the class Magnoliopsida and the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Benkara miquelii, also known as the Benkara plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Benkara miquelii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Benkara miquelii is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound healing.
